revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従三位]][[ko:종3위]][[zh:從三位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従三位]][[ko:종3위]][[zh:從三位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従三位]][[ko:종3위]][[zh:從三位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従三位]][[ko:종3위]][[zh:從三位]]
The '''Junior Third Rank (従三位, Jusanmi)''' is one of the Ranks in the '''[[Court Ranks in Japan|Court Rank System]] (位階)''' and the '''[[Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinto Divine Rank System]] (神階).'''
It is positioned below the '''{{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} (正三位)''' and above the '''{{ill|Senior Fourth Rank|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Upper Grade (正四位上).''' Under the '''[[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] (律令制),''' officials holding the position of '''Councilor (参議)''' and those Ranked '''Junior Third Rank''' or higher were referred to as '''{{ill|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3138036}} (Kugyō).''' In cases of {{ill|posthumous Rank|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/posthumous Rank|1=Zoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97379040}}, the title was conferred as '''Zō Jusanmi (贈従三位).'''<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, individuals holding the Rank of Jusanmi or higher were called '''Ki''' (貴), and it was sometimes referred to as the "star Rank." It was a high {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} court Rank. In comparison with {{ill|Kunto|ja|勤等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was equivalent to {{ill|Kunto ni-to|ja|勤二等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunto ni-to|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those who held the court position of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|qid=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} at the Rank of {{ill|Shishii|ja|正四位上|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}} or higher, including Jusanmi, were regarded as {{ill|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3138036}} or {{ill|Jotatsubo|ja|上達部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jotatsubo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公卿|1=Kugyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3138036}}.
Holders of Jusanmi or higher used the title "{{ill|Ason|en|Ason|qid=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}}" (朝臣) as a surname following their {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|qid=Q2575910|lt=uji|1=Uji (clan)}} (氏) and received the honorific "{{ill|Kyō|ja|卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō|1=Kyou (officials)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10910812}}" (卿) after their {{ill|name|ja|謫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/name|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (謫). Those appointed to the {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|qid=Q83307|lt=Daijin|1=Minister (government)}} position received the honorific "{{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131554777|lt=Kō|1=Kō (kabane)}}" (公). In contrast, individuals holding the Rank of {{ill|Shii|ja|四位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shii|1=Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11419606}} (四位) or higher but not part of Kugyō were called "Asomi" without additional honorifics. Furthermore, upon the death of a Kugyō of Sanmi Rank or higher, their passing was referred to as "{{ill|Kōkyo|ja|薩去|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (薩去), similar to the terminology used for {{ill|Imperial family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581068}} members. Later periods saw the title "Sanmi-dono" (三位殿) or "Sanmi-sama" (三位様) used as a form of address.
Additionally, those holding the Rank of Sanmi or higher were permitted to establish their own {{ill|household administration|ja|家政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/household administration|1=household affairs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11455459}} office, referred to as '''{{ill|Mandokoro|en|Mandokoro|qid=Q2279768|lt=Mandokoro|1=Mandokoro}}''' (政所). Key positions within these offices included "Shokujisanmi Karei" (職事審三位家令) and "Shōshoi Shokujisanmi Kasho-ri" (小初位下職事三位家書里).
In the {{ill|kan-i sōtō|ja|官位相対|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kan-i sōtō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (官位相当) system, positions corresponding to Jusanmi included {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|大宿布帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Danjō no In|ja|弾正布帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjō no In|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中統議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūnagon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Konoefu|ja|近衛布|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoefu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (左右の近衛大帝). However, in practice, Dazai no Sōchi and Danjō no In were often held by {{ill|imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}}, while the positions of the Lieutenant Commanders of the Guards were conventionally held by the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|qid=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} (左大臣), {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|qid=Q1351174|lt=Udaijin|1=Minister of the Right}} (右大臣), or {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大統議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainagon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making them typically Ranked {{ill|Shosanmi|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shosanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} or higher. Those at the Rank of Jusanmi often served as {{ill|Konoe-chūjō|ja|近衛中帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe-chūjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Individuals holding the Rank of Sanmi but serving as Chūjō were referred to as '''Sanmi Chūjō''' (三位中帝).
== Historical Context ==
During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, before [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}} became the first {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|qid=Q38142|lt=samurai|1=Samurai}} to enter the Ranks of the Kugyō, the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|qid=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa Genji}} and {{ill|Kanmu Heishi|ja|桑武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clans had the {{ill|Shishii|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Rank as their highest Rank. Therefore, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimasa|en|Minamoto no Yorimasa|qid=Q164948|lt=Minamoto no Yorimasa|1=Minamoto no Yorimasa}} of the {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|赤須源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attained the Rank of Jusanmi as the first {{ill|buke|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/buke|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} samurai to do so, it was considered unprecedented and led to him being popularly referred to as "Gen Sanmi" (源三位).
During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|宦平時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Rank of Jusanmi was conferred upon various individuals, including members of the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利帝府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage, {{ill|Kamakura kubō|ja|釜倉公斧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura kubō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and prominent {{ill|kanrei|ja|粋領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kanrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} families such as the {{ill|Shiba clan|ja|早巻氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Modern Era ==
Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治積極|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Rank was retained under the {{ill|Imperial Court Rank system|ja|尊官位階制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court Rank system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On May 6, 1887, the {{ill|Joi Ordinance|ja|司位伍位制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Ordinance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} equated the Ranks of Shosanmi and Jusanmi with the {{ill|viscount|ja|小宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} title in terms of social prestige.
== References ==
{{Court Ranks of Japan}} *
{{translated page|ja|従三位|version=104590404|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:従三位]]
[[ja:審三位]]

First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Ninth Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Third Rank--><!--Junior Third Rank--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:종3위]] [[zh:從三位]] {{wikidata link|Q11071123}} [[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
